main_paper.raw: {"methods":["SQuBa: Speech Querying Mamba Architecture (end-to-end model)","Query-attention Mamba projector for compressing acoustic features","Windowing Q-Mamba for selective compression via cross-attention and learnable queries","Two-stage training framework","Bootstrapped Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) fine-tuning"],"problems":["Efficient abstractive speech summarization for long speech inputs","Reducing computational and memory overhead in speech summarization models","Bridging the modality gap between speech and text for summarization","Improving inference speed for real-world speech summarization applications"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Inference speed","Summarization performance (e.g., ROUGE, but not explicitly mentioned in the provided text)"],"results":[{"metric":"Inference speed","value":"Significantly improved compared to transformer-based approaches"},{"metric":"Summarization performance","value":"Competitive with state-of-the-art (exact numbers not provided in the text)"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of a query-attention Mamba projector to compress long speech prompts into compact semantic tokens, reducing computational footprint.","Extension of Mamba-based LLMs to efficiently handle long speech inputs for summarization.","A two-stage training process including bootstrapped DPO fine-tuning for improved summarization quality and efficiency.","Empirical demonstration of competitive performance with much faster inference compared to transformer-based models."]}

methods: SQuBa: Speech Querying Mamba Architecture (end-to-end model)
Query-attention Mamba projector for compressing acoustic features
Windowing Q-Mamba for selective compression via cross-attention and learnable queries
Two-stage training framework
Bootstrapped Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) fine-tuning
problems: Efficient abstractive speech summarization for long speech inputs
Reducing computational and memory overhead in speech summarization models
Bridging the modality gap between speech and text for summarization
Improving inference speed for real-world speech summarization applications

results.value: Significantly improved compared to transformer-based approaches
Competitive with state-of-the-art (exact numbers not provided in the text)

novelty_claims: Introduction of a query-attention Mamba projector to compress long speech prompts into compact semantic tokens, reducing computational footprint.
Extension of Mamba-based LLMs to efficiently handle long speech inputs for summarization.
A two-stage training process including bootstrapped DPO fine-tuning for improved summarization quality and efficiency.
Empirical demonstration of competitive performance with much faster inference compared to transformer-based models.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["End-to-end speech summarization model using Q-Former as an audio-text connector and LLMs for summary generation","Multi-stage training approach including LLM-based ASR and Text Summarization (TSum) as auxiliary tasks","Curriculum learning strategy to transition from TSum to SSum","Segment-level speech encoding with Q-Former to handle long speech inputs","Efficient parameter fine-tuning of LLMs for speech summarization"],"problems":["Difficulty in handling long speech input for summarization","Capturing complex cross-modal mapping between long speech and short text summaries","Modality gap between audio (speech) and text outputs","Computational complexity of encoding long speech sequences","Error propagation in cascaded ASR+TSum systems"],"datasets":["How2"],"metrics":["BERTScore"],"results":[{"metric":"BERTScore","value":"Model achieves performance exceeding cascaded models and comparable to strong end-to-end baselines (exact value not specified)"}],"novelty_claims":["First to propose an end-to-end speech summarization model that directly leverages LLMs with Q-Former for audio-text modality connection","Introduces a multi-stage training strategy combining ASR and TSum auxiliary tasks to enhance LLM's ability to process long speech and bridge modality gap","Employs curriculum learning to facilitate transition from text summarization to speech summarization","Demonstrates competitive performance on the How2 dataset, surpassing cascaded models and matching strong end-to-end baselines"]}

methods: End-to-end speech summarization model using Q-Former as an audio-text connector and LLMs for summary generation
Multi-stage training approach including LLM-based ASR and Text Summarization (TSum) as auxiliary tasks
Curriculum learning strategy to transition from TSum to SSum
Segment-level speech encoding with Q-Former to handle long speech inputs
Efficient parameter fine-tuning of LLMs for speech summarization
problems: Difficulty in handling long speech input for summarization
Capturing complex cross-modal mapping between long speech and short text summaries
Modality gap between audio (speech) and text outputs
Computational complexity of encoding long speech sequences
Error propagation in cascaded ASR+TSum systems

results.value: Model achieves performance exceeding cascaded models and comparable to strong end-to-end baselines (exact value not specified)

novelty_claims: First to propose an end-to-end speech summarization model that directly leverages LLMs with Q-Former for audio-text modality connection
Introduces a multi-stage training strategy combining ASR and TSum auxiliary tasks to enhance LLM's ability to process long speech and bridge modality gap
Employs curriculum learning to facilitate transition from text summarization to speech summarization
Demonstrates competitive performance on the How2 dataset, surpassing cascaded models and matching strong end-to-end baselines

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["End-to-end system combining an instruction-tuned large language model (LLM) with an audio encoder that converts speech into token representations interpretable by the LLM","Training the audio encoder with paired speech-text data to achieve modality invariance, so the LLM produces consistent responses regardless of input modality","Using next-token prediction loss to guide the system to produce the same output for speech and text inputs","Incorporating knowledge distillation from the text input response as a teacher model, using feature and logit distillation losses"],"problems":["General-purpose speech summarization from audio inputs","Overcoming domain and style limitations of prior end-to-end speech summarization systems","Reducing errors from the cascade of ASR followed by text summarization","Enabling LLMs to process and summarize speech inputs directly"],"datasets":["CNN / DailyMail"],"metrics":["Response language modeling","Speech summarization quality"],"results":[{"metric":"Speech summarization quality (on CNN / DailyMail)","value":"Proposed system outperforms cascade baseline of ASR + LLM text processing"}],"novelty_claims":["First framework to enable an LLM to process and summarize arbitrary speech inputs by converting speech to token representations the LLM can interpret","Achieves modality invariance so the LLM can respond consistently to prompts regardless of input modality (speech or text)","Allows for general-purpose speech summarization across arbitrary domains and in different summary styles by varying the LLM prompt","Outperforms traditional cascade approaches (ASR + LLM) in both response language modeling and speech summarization tasks"]}

methods: End-to-end system combining an instruction-tuned large language model (LLM) with an audio encoder that converts speech into token representations interpretable by the LLM
Training the audio encoder with paired speech-text data to achieve modality invariance, so the LLM produces consistent responses regardless of input modality
Using next-token prediction loss to guide the system to produce the same output for speech and text inputs
Incorporating knowledge distillation from the text input response as a teacher model, using feature and logit distillation losses
problems: General-purpose speech summarization from audio inputs
Overcoming domain and style limitations of prior end-to-end speech summarization systems
Reducing errors from the cascade of ASR followed by text summarization
Enabling LLMs to process and summarize speech inputs directly

results.value: Proposed system outperforms cascade baseline of ASR + LLM text processing

novelty_claims: First framework to enable an LLM to process and summarize arbitrary speech inputs by converting speech to token representations the LLM can interpret
Achieves modality invariance so the LLM can respond consistently to prompts regardless of input modality (speech or text)
Allows for general-purpose speech summarization across arbitrary domains and in different summary styles by varying the LLM prompt
Outperforms traditional cascade approaches (ASR + LLM) in both response language modeling and speech summarization tasks

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Block-wise Adaptation for Speech Summarization (BASS)","Block-wise training for speech summarization","Passing latent semantic representations across blocks","Streaming/incremental summary refinement after each input block"],"problems":["Difficulty of training end-to-end speech summarization models on very long audio inputs due to computational constraints","Performance degradation due to input truncation in speech summarization models","Need for effective summarization of long-form speech (e.g., lectures, podcasts)"],"datasets":["How2 dataset"],"metrics":["ROUGE-L"],"results":[{"metric":"ROUGE-L","value":"BASS improves by 3 points absolute over a truncated input baseline"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of BASS, a novel block-wise adaptation algorithm for speech summarization that predicts and refines summaries incrementally as new audio blocks are processed","Use of an explicit semantic representation layer to aggregate and carry semantic context across input blocks","Demonstration that block-wise adaptation from a pretrained model with BASS improves ROUGE-L by 3 points over truncated input baselines"]}

methods: Block-wise Adaptation for Speech Summarization (BASS)
Block-wise training for speech summarization
Passing latent semantic representations across blocks
Streaming/incremental summary refinement after each input block
problems: Difficulty of training end-to-end speech summarization models on very long audio inputs due to computational constraints
Performance degradation due to input truncation in speech summarization models
Need for effective summarization of long-form speech (e.g., lectures, podcasts)

results.value: BASS improves by 3 points absolute over a truncated input baseline

novelty_claims: Introduction of BASS, a novel block-wise adaptation algorithm for speech summarization that predicts and refines summaries incrementally as new audio blocks are processed
Use of an explicit semantic representation layer to aggregate and carry semantic context across input blocks
Demonstration that block-wise adaptation from a pretrained model with BASS improves ROUGE-L by 3 points over truncated input baselines

methods: End-to-end (E2E) speech summarization system capable of processing up to 10-minute speech sequences

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["End-to-end speech summarization model using restricted self-attention","Application of restricted self-attention (from text-based models) to speech models"],"problems":["High memory and compute requirements for end-to-end speech summarization due to long input audio sequences","Error propagation and complexity in cascade architectures for speech summarization (ASR followed by text summarization)"],"datasets":["How-2 corpus (How-2 2000h Dataset)"],"metrics":["ROUGE","F-1 score"],"results":[{"metric":"ROUGE","value":"+3 points absolute over cascade model"},{"metric":"F-1 score (concept prediction)","value":"+4 points absolute over cascade model"}],"novelty_claims":["First to introduce a single end-to-end model for speech summarization using restricted self-attention to address long input sequence challenges.","Demonstrates the effectiveness of restricted self-attention for speech inputs, enabling end-to-end speech summarization.","Shows that the end-to-end model outperforms traditional cascade models on both summarization and spoken language understanding tasks."]}

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Directly fine-tuned LLMs (full and LoRA parameter fine-tuning)","Superposed LLMs and VALL-E (LLM encodes text and acoustic tokens, then fed to VALL-E)","Coupled LLMs and VALL-E (LLM as text encoder, output to VALL-E AR model)","Discretization of speech into multi-layer codec codes using Encodec","Expanding LLM vocabulary with codec code tokens"],"problems":["How to effectively augment large language models (LLMs) with speech synthesis capabilities","Whether codec codes can be treated as a language by LLMs","What information LLMs can provide to improve synthesized speech quality"],"datasets":["Multilingual Librispeech English (44.5K hours)","LibriSpeech (960 hours)","LibriSpeech dev-clean","LibriSpeech dev-other","LibriSpeech test-clean","LibriSpeech test-other"],"metrics":["Speaker similarity","Word Error Rate (WER)"],"results":[{"metric":"Word Error Rate (WER) reduction","value":"10.9% reduction compared to original speech synthesis models"},{"metric":"Speaker similarity","value":"Consistently better than original speech synthesis models (exact value not specified)"}],"novelty_claims":["First comprehensive empirical study comparing multiple integration strategies (direct fine-tuning, superposed, coupled) for boosting LLMs with speech synthesis capability.","Demonstrates that directly fine-tuning LLMs for codec code generation is ineffective, and that using LLMs as a text encoder coupled with VALL-E achieves superior speech synthesis performance.","Shows that LLMs can provide valuable representations for codec language models, improving both speaker similarity and WER in generated speech.","Provides analysis on the impact of model size, pre-training, and fine-tuning strategies for speech synthesis with LLMs."]}

methods: Directly fine-tuned LLMs (full and LoRA parameter fine-tuning)
Superposed LLMs and VALL-E (LLM encodes text and acoustic tokens, then fed to VALL-E)
Coupled LLMs and VALL-E (LLM as text encoder, output to VALL-E AR model)
Discretization of speech into multi-layer codec codes using Encodec
Expanding LLM vocabulary with codec code tokens
problems: How to effectively augment large language models (LLMs) with speech synthesis capabilities
Whether codec codes can be treated as a language by LLMs
What information LLMs can provide to improve synthesized speech quality

results.value: 10.9% reduction compared to original speech synthesis models
Consistently better than original speech synthesis models (exact value not specified)

novelty_claims: First comprehensive empirical study comparing multiple integration strategies (direct fine-tuning, superposed, coupled) for boosting LLMs with speech synthesis capability.
Demonstrates that directly fine-tuning LLMs for codec code generation is ineffective, and that using LLMs as a text encoder coupled with VALL-E achieves superior speech synthesis performance.
Shows that LLMs can provide valuable representations for codec language models, improving both speaker similarity and WER in generated speech.
Provides analysis on the impact of model size, pre-training, and fine-tuning strategies for speech synthesis with LLMs.

methods: Replacing Transformer-based backbone in MLLMs with pre-trained Mamba language models (state-space models) for linear scalability and efficient inference
Integrating visual encoders (e.g., DINOv2, SigLIP) with Mamba LLMs via a projector that maps visual features to the language embedding space
Empirical exploration of multi-modal fusion strategies for aligning visual and textual modalities
Fine-tuning a subset (~48%) of model parameters for improved efficiency and performance
problems: Inefficiency of Transformer-based MLLMs due to quadratic computational complexity, limiting real-time and edge deployment

results.value: Cobra performs 3× to 4× faster than LLaVA-Phi and MobileVLM v2-3B

Cobra achieves comparable or better performance than LLaVA v1.5 with only ~48% of the parameters

Proposes and empirically evaluates new multi-modal fusion strategies for integrating visual and language modalities in a state-space model-based MLLM
Shows that fine-tuning a smaller subset of parameters can yield significant performance improvements in MLLMs

novelty_claims: Demonstrates that fully diagonal state space models (DSS) can match the performance of the more complex S4 model, which uses a diagonal plus low-rank structure, on long-range reasoning tasks.
Shows that the low-rank correction in S4 is not necessary for high performance; diagonal matrices suffice if properly initialized.
DSS is conceptually simpler, easier to implement, and analyze compared to S4, requiring only basic linear algebra and fitting in a single page of code.
Provides empirical evidence that DSS outperforms Transformer variants and matches S4 on challenging long-range tasks across multiple modalities.

methods: Evaluation of in-context learning (ICL) capabilities of state-space models (SSMs), specifically Mamba, compared to Transformers

Training models specifically for ICL tasks following the approach of Garg et al. (2022)
problems: Can attention-free models (like SSMs/Mamba) perform in-context learning (ICL) as effectively as Transformers?
What are the strengths and weaknesses of SSMs (Mamba) versus Transformers on various ICL tasks?

novelty_claims: First comprehensive evaluation of in-context learning (ICL) capabilities of state-space models (SSMs), specifically Mamba, compared to Transformers across a diverse set of ICL tasks.

Introduction of MambaFormer, a novel hybrid architecture combining Mamba and attention blocks, which eliminates the need for positional encodings and achieves best-of-both-worlds performance on ICL tasks.
Demonstration that SSMs can match or outperform Transformers on certain ICL tasks, challenging the assumption that attention is necessary for ICL.

Proposal of two novel tasks: audio-based storytelling and speech audio co-reasoning.

methods: Token-level Direct Preference Optimization (TDPO)
Incorporation of forward KL divergence constraints at the token level
Token-based reward system using the Bradley-Terry model
problems: Aligning large language models (LLMs) with human preferences at a fine-grained (token) level

TDPO offers improved regulation and efficiency over DPO

novelty_claims: Proposes Token-level Direct Preference Optimization (TDPO), a new method for aligning LLMs with human preferences by optimizing at the token level rather than the sentence/response level.
TDPO introduces forward KL divergence constraints for each token, improving both alignment and generative diversity.
TDPO uses a token-based reward system via the Bradley-Terry model, avoiding explicit reward modeling.
TDPO maintains the simplicity of DPO while providing better regulation of KL divergence and improved divergence efficiency.
TDPO demonstrates superior performance in balancing alignment and diversity compared to DPO and PPO-based RLHF methods.
